$schema_note: CANONICAL, SINGLE SOURCE of all global fleet metadata for ClickUp Super Agents. Every structured fact about an agent (identity, track, version, status, channels, lane, triggers) lives HERE and nowhere else. Per-agent folders hold ONLY non-duplicated content: preferences.md (profile), memory.md (context), activity-log.md (ledger), decision-log.md (reasoning), README.md (steward pointer), audits/ (dated records). Never hand-mirror any field below into a folder file. index.html renders this; index.md points at both.

defined_by: ClickUp Super Agent — Creation & Setup Checklist (docs/12cwjm-54133/12cwjm-74773)
Golden Config Skeleton — Copy-Me Starting Instructions (docs/12cwjm-54133/12cwjm-74793)

git_teammate_standard.note: git-teammate track = session-invocable persona via /session.agent=<Name>, rides on the full Brain stack, holds memory across sessions. NOT a native ClickUp Super Agent (no autonomous triggers).

agents.creator: Michael Wizorek

agents.notes: Fleet Steward. Reference implementation for v1.0.

status_legend.up-to-date: Full-Standard agent verified against current golden-standard version.
status_legend.needs-declaration: Full-Standard agent whose declaration folder and/or verbatim preferences.md mirror is not yet complete.
status_legend.needs-re-audit: Standard bumped or config changed; re-verify.
status_legend.behind: Known gaps against the current golden standard.
status_legend.inventory-only: Task-Specific / Exempt; tracked but not held to the full standard.

changelog: 2026-07-19: registered workhorse-wes as the first git-teammate track (active); added git_teammate_standard pointer block (runtime base spec + authoring gate). Wes migrated from brain-config/agents/ into the fleet with the full 4-file bundle; old lens file redirected.
